basic_info.name: Uluwatu (Chinese: 烏魯瓦圖; Indonesian: Uluwatu)
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~60-75 min by motorbike (~30-35 km); From Uluwatu town: 0 min (this is the namesake spot, ~5 min from Pecatu)

surf_conditions.wave_type: Left-hand point/reef break (world-class). Multiple peaks along same reef: Outside Corner, The Peak (Main Peak), Racetracks, Temples, The Bombie, Inside
surf_conditions.skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced/Pro (Temples inside section is the most forgiving; Outside Corner is Pro-only on 8ft+ days)
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: 3-15 ft (most consistent 4-8 ft; Outside Corner switches on at 8 ft+; epic at 6-10 ft)
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October (peak June-August); shoulder season late September-October offers best value with consistent swell and fewer crowds
surf_conditions.best_tide: Mid to high tide for inner peaks (Peak, Racetracks, Temples); low tide for Outside Corner. Surfable on all tides depending on section.
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore SE (Southeast) trade winds typical in dry season
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) groundswell; also handles S and SSW
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (ultra-consistent; one of the most reliable spots on the planet during dry season)
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: 100-300+ meters depending on section and swell; long rides linking sections possible on bigger days [uncertain]
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Medium to fast (Racetracks section is notably fast and hollow; Temples is slower and more forgiving)
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate to high difficulty; steep drop on bigger days especially at Outside Corner and The Peak; shallow reef under takeoff zone requires precise positioning
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: Yes - Outside Corner (outside), The Peak (main), Racetracks (south section), Temples (inside/closer to cliff), The Bombie (offshore big-wave peak), Inside
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 5 (very crowded, especially when surf is small/head-high); thins out at 6 ft+. Most crowded 9am-4pm in dry season.

practical_info.access: Drive to clifftop parking at end of Jl. Mamo (off Jl. Labuansait), walk ~200m down stairs through warungs/shops on cliff face, then narrow stairs through the iconic cave to reef/beach. Three staircase options as backup for big days.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Exit cave at high tide and paddle straight out through the channel; at low tide walk ~100m across sharp reef before paddling. Channel between Peak and Racetracks is the main paddle-out channel.
practical_info.entry_fee: Parking: ~IDR 5,000-10,000 motorbike, IDR 10,000-20,000 car; no surf entry fee [uncertain]
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy conditions and lighter crowds; sunset session for golden-hour photos
practical_info.best_time_of_day: Before 8 AM (dawn patrol) to avoid crowds; or late afternoon after 4 PM when day-trippers leave
practical_info.nearby_warung: Numerous warungs and cafes along the cliff stairs (Single Fin, Delpi Rock Lounge, Mango Tree Cafe, Suka Espresso, Drifter Surf Cafe nearby in Pecatu)
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Padang Padang (5 min), Bingin (10 min), Impossibles (10 min), Balangan (15 min by motorbike)
practical_info.boat_charter_required: No - land access via stairs and cave

safety_culture.hazards: Sharp shallow reef, strong currents, crowded lineup, waves pounding cliff face at high tide near cave entry, reef cuts, occasional sea urchins
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef (wear booties), occasional jellyfish, rare sea snakes; treat reef cuts immediately with antiseptic and check tetanus status; no shark issues
safety_culture.reef_cut_severity_index: High - sharp limestone/coral karang reef; booties strongly recommended especially at low tide and through Racetracks section
safety_culture.rip_current_pattern: Strong rip current runs from inside reef out through the channel between The Peak and Racetracks - useful for paddle-out but dangerous if caught while exiting. Currents drag surfers south toward Padang Padang on big days.
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Strict priority system, wait your turn, do not paddle around or snake locals. Smile and say hi to locals. Do not drop in - locals will call you out. Be patient when crowded.
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Medium to High - lineup is competitive especially at The Peak; respect Balinese locals and long-term expats; localism more pronounced on epic swell days
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Show respect to local Balinese surfers, do not snake or burn waves, wait your turn, acknowledge priority, no dropping in. Avoid arrogance - many locals are pro-level.
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Mega Semadhi (2016 Rip Curl Cup champion, Bingin/Bukit local), Rizal Tandjung, Made Switra, Marlon Gerber, Bol Adyaksa, Garut Widiarta - many of Indonesia's top surfers grew up surfing the Bukit
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Pura Luhur Uluwatu (cliff temple) sits directly above; respect sacred site, dress modestly when visiting temple, do not climb temple walls. Nyepi (Day of Silence, typically March) - no surfing or beach access allowed islandwide. Avoid surfing during temple ceremonies.
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Unicare Pecatu Clinic (closest, ~5-10 min); BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (~25-30 min); Siloam Hospital Denpasar (~45 min)
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski permanently stationed; safety patrols during Rip Curl Cup event only
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; Ambulance: 118/119; BIMC Nusa Dua: +62 361 3000 911

services.nearest_surf_school: Padang Padang Surf Camp (Pecatu), Kala Surf Camp (Jl. Labuansait 318, Pecatu), Bali Bisa Surf School, Dawn Patrol Bali, WaveHouse Bali, Bali Surf Pro - most operate beginner lessons at Balangan/Padang Padang Right rather than Uluwatu itself
services.board_rental_availability: Yes - widely available; Bali Surfboard Rental and multiple shops along Jl. Labuansait/Suluban. ~IDR 200,000/day, IDR 1,000,000/week, IDR 3,000,000/month
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - multiple land and water photographers shoot daily at The Peak; freelance photographers stationed at cliff cafes (Single Fin) and at cave
services.photographer_recommendation: Padang Padang Surf Camp photography service; freelance shooters along the cliff. Photos typically IDR 100,000-300,000 per shot or IDR 500,000-1,500,000 per session [uncertain]
services.accommodation_options: Uluwatu Surf Villas (clifftop boutique, $150-800+/night), Kala Surf Camp (from IDR 978,000/night with coaching), Padang Padang Surf Camp, Dreamsea Bali Uluwatu, WaveHouse Bali, Mu Bali, The Edge Bali, numerous mid-range homestays on Jl. Labuansait
services.drone_allowed: Generally allowed for personal use but increasingly restricted by Indonesian regulations; commercial drone work requires permit. Avoid flying over Uluwatu Temple (sacred site). [uncertain]
services.coworking_nearby: Outpost Ulu Pecatu, Tropical Nomad (Canggu - too far), B Work Bali Pecatu, Bukit Cafe (laptop-friendly), Suka Espresso (laptop-friendly)

pricing.avg_lesson_price: USD 25-50 / IDR 400,000-800,000 per group lesson (most beginner lessons held at Balangan or Padang Padang Right, not Uluwatu main peak)
pricing.board_rental_daily: IDR 150,000-250,000/day (~USD 10-17) for shortboards; performance boards may cost more

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Good in dry season (May-October); declines significantly in wet season (Nov-April) due to runoff and sewage from cliff developments. Pecatu/Bukit has minimal river runoff so cleaner than west coast Canggu.

events_community.annual_competition_window: Rip Curl Cup Padang Padang (held at neighboring Padang Padang, not Uluwatu itself) - holding period August 1 to early September each year, waiting for solid 6-8 ft+ swell. Single Fin Sunday parties at Single Fin bar above the cave year-round.
events_community.community_events: Single Fin Sunday (weekly sunset party at Single Fin bar); Rip Curl Cup viewing parties in August; occasional Volcom and local brand events; full-moon ceremonies at Uluwatu Temple
